claim: The what-now harness shall surface a CLI-only action for each DECIDED conflict whose revisit_marker has never been evaluated or was last evaluated more than the staleness delta of SETTLED atoms ago.
why: Not yet ported in the Go port. A DECIDED conflict's revisit_marker names the CONDITION under which the decision should be re-opened (the historian's anti-relitigation trigger), but nothing tracked whether anyone LOOKED at it again -- an unread trigger lets the decision silently ossify while its stated revisit condition may already have come true. The historical Python writer was tools/mark_revisit_evaluated.py (appending a {stamp, conflict, settled_count} record to spec/.runtime/revisit-eval.jsonl when the resolver evaluates a marker); tools/what_now.py then read the last evaluation per conflict and re-surfaced the marker as 'evaluate revisit marker' when never evaluated or stale by more than GENERATIVE_AUDIT_STALE_DELTA SETTLED atoms. In the Go port mark-revisit-evaluated is Declared (not ported) in methodology.Tools, and the what-now harness (cmd/hotam/what_now.go over internal/diagnose/signal.go) renders only graph-derived signals -- there is no revisit-evaluation band reading the filesystem (stays OUT of the signal set for the same determinism reason as the pending-proposal / generative-audit bands: it reads the filesystem, which gen_spec must not render). The conflict.RevisitMarker field itself is carried and written by ProposedConflictTransition.mutate, so the marker survives in substrate; only the evaluation-tracking reader/writer is unported. Evaluating a marker is an OBSERVATION; re-opening the conflict is a separate ProposedConflictTransition the resolver drafts (R-ai-presents-not-decides). PROSE (not ENFORCED): no enforcer exists in the Go port.
